description: An integer truncation vulnerability exists in the Maglev JIT compiler's node representation. The `NodeBase` class in `maglev-ir.h` stores the number of inputs for each IR node in a 16-bit bitfield (`InputCountField`). When allocating a node via `NodeBase::Allocate`, the provided `input_count` is encoded into this 16-bit field using `InputCountField::encode(input_count)`, which has no runtime overflow check in release builds (only a DCHECK). The `GeneratorStore` node, built in `VisitSuspendGenerator` in `maglev-graph-builder.cc`, requires one input per function parameter and one per live local variable in a generator function. When a generator function contains a sufficiently large number of local variables (>= 65536), the computed `input_count` overflows the 16-bit field upon encoding, resulting in a truncated value stored in the bitfield. This creates a mismatch between the actual number of `ValueNode*` inputs physically allocated (and thus the memory layout of the node) and the `input_count` returned by `NodeBase::input_count()`. During register allocation, `GeneratorStore::SetValueLocationConstraints` iterates over `num_parameters_and_registers()` inputs (derived from the truncated count), accessing locations beyond the legitimately allocated input array, causing memory corruption (use-after-poison as reported by ASan).

fixes.evidence_text: 84c0b167d973cdeba2f57004fd4d500e70a8b992 [maglev] SBXCHECK input count is less than max allowed
fixes.description: Local patch exported from https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/v8/v8/+/6939005 for reproducible fixed-image builds.
